## Prior-predictive check

Does the prior generate physically-possible outcomes before seeing the data
(Gabry et al. 2019)? The prior-predictive distribution of the outcome count is
overlaid on the observed counts. _How to read it:_ the prior-predictive spread
(blue) should comfortably cover the observed counts (red) without being wildly
wider — if the observed data sit in the extreme tails the prior is too tight; if
the blue dwarfs the red the prior is barely informative.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# For the correlated-factor measurement model the y_post prior draws are NOT
# data-free: the factory reconstructs the factor scores from the observed
# indicator matrix (cond_mean = Z_d @ W.T), so the y_post prior/posterior
# predictive conditions on the observed indicators (issue #271).
if config.get("kind") == "corr_factor":
    print(
        "_Note:_ for this measurement model the `y_post` prior draws are **not** "
        "data-free — the factor scores are reconstructed from the observed "
        "indicator matrix, so this prior-predictive (and the posterior-predictive) "
        "conditions on the observed indicators rather than generating outcomes from "
        "the prior alone.\n"
    )
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("prior_predictive_check.png", "Prior-predictive vs observed"))
if not _has("prior_predictive_check.png"):
    print("_No prior-predictive plot available for this model._")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
# Floored outcomes (P / N): can the prior reproduce the observed ~64% t2 floor?
# Baseline zero rates are about 78% for P and 72% for N. This plot diagnoses the
# post-hoc binary off-floor branch; it does not establish prior specification.
print(_img("prior_proportion_at_zero.png", "Prior proportion-at-zero check"))
```
